301.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Umayr mawla Abi al-Lahm (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performing ruqya — seeking protection for the sick — with his hands.
301.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Umayr mawla Abi al-Lahm (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ melakukan ruqyah — memohon perlindungan untuk orang yang sakit — dengan tangannya.'

302.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rifa'a ibn Shaddad (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I used to stand guard over al-Mukhtar. When I came to know of his lying, I was determined — by Allah — to draw my sword and strike his neck. Then I remembered a hadith of 'Amr ibn al-Hamiq al-Khuza'i: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: «Whoever gives security to a man and then kills him, I am free of the one who does the killing, even if the victim was a disbeliever.»'
302.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rifa'ah ibn Syaddad (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku biasa berdiri menjaga al-Mukhtar. Ketika aku mengetahui kebohongannya, aku bertekad — demi Allah — untuk mencabut pedangku dan memukul lehernya. Kemudian aku teringat sebuah hadits dari 'Amr ibn al-Hamiq al-Khuza'i: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: «Barangsiapa memberi keamanan kepada seseorang kemudian membunuhnya, maka aku berlepas diri dari orang yang membunuh itu, meskipun korbannya adalah orang kafir.»'

303.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rifa'a al-Qitbani (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I entered upon al-Mukhtar and he threw me a cushion and said: «If my brother Jibril had not just risen from this cushion, I would have offered it to you.» I said: By Allah, this is lying. Then I left.'
303.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rifa'ah al-Qithbani (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku masuk kepada al-Mukhtar dan ia melemparkan sebuah bantal kepadaku dan berkata: «Seandainya saudaraku Jibril tidak baru saja bangkit dari bantal ini, aku akan melemparkannya untukmu.» Aku berkata: Demi Allah, ini adalah kebohongan. Lalu aku pergi.'

304.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rifa'a ibn Shaddad (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I used to stand guard over al-Mukhtar. When I recognized his lying, I was determined to draw my sword and strike his neck. Then I remembered a hadith from 'Amr ibn al-Hamiq: the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: «Whoever gives security to a man by blood or property and then violates it, upon him is the curse of Allah, the angels, and all the people.»'
304.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rifa'ah ibn Syaddad (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku biasa berdiri menjaga al-Mukhtar. Ketika aku mengenali kebohongannya, aku bertekad untuk mencabut pedangku dan memukul lehernya. Kemudian aku teringat sebuah hadits dari 'Amr ibn al-Hamiq: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: «Barangsiapa memberikan keamanan kepada seseorang melalui darah atau harta kemudian melanggarnya, maka atasnya laknat Allah, para malaikat, dan seluruh manusia.»'

305.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn al-Hamiq al-Khuza'i (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Prophet ﷺ say: 'When Allah wills good for a ruler, He appoints for him a truthful minister who reminds him if he forgets and helps him if he remembers. And when He wills otherwise, He appoints for him an evil minister who does not remind him if he forgets and does not help him if he remembers.'
305.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn al-Hamiq al-Khuza'i (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila Allah menghendaki kebaikan bagi seorang pemimpin, Dia mengangkat untuknya seorang menteri yang jujur yang mengingatkannya jika ia lupa dan membantunya jika ia ingat. Dan apabila Dia menghendaki yang lain, Dia mengangkat untuknya seorang menteri yang buruk yang tidak mengingatkannya jika ia lupa dan tidak membantunya jika ia ingat.'

306.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded Abdullah ibn Rawaha to look into a matter of distribution, and he did so. A man said to him: 'You have been unjust.' Abdullah ibn Rawaha raised his hand to strike him, then restrained himself, and said: 'By Allah, you have harmed one whom Allah has honored, and honored one whom Allah has harmed.'
306.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang laki-laki di antara sahabat Nabi ﷺ bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan Abdullah ibn Rawahah untuk memeriksa suatu pembagian, dan ia melakukannya. Seorang laki-laki berkata kepadanya: 'Kamu telah berlaku tidak adil.' Abdullah ibn Rawahah mengangkat tangannya untuk memukulnya, kemudian ia menahan diri, dan berkata: 'Demi Allah, kamu telah menyakiti orang yang dimuliakan Allah, dan memuliakan orang yang dihinakan Allah.'

307.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man among the Companions of the Prophet ﷺ that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The best of witnesses is one who gives testimony before being asked for it.'
307.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang laki-laki di antara sahabat Nabi ﷺ bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Sebaik-baik saksi adalah orang yang memberikan kesaksian sebelum diminta.'

308.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sadusi — Ibn al-Khasasiyya (may Allah be pleased with him) — that he said: 'I went to the Prophet ﷺ to pledge allegiance to him. He stipulated upon me witnessing that there is no god but Allah and that Muhammad is His Messenger, establishing prayer, paying zakat, performing Hajj, fasting Ramadan, and striving in the way of Allah. I said: O Messenger of Allah, I agree to all of it except zakat — for I have only ten camels which are my riding animals and provisions. He accepted my pledge with that exception.'
308.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sadusi — Ibn al-Khasasiyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku pergi kepada Nabi ﷺ untuk berbaiat kepadanya. Beliau menetapkan syarat kepadaku: bersaksi bahwa tidak ada tuhan selain Allah dan Muhammad adalah utusan-Nya, mendirikan shalat, membayar zakat, melaksanakan haji, berpuasa Ramadan, dan berjihad di jalan Allah. Aku berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, aku setuju dengan semuanya kecuali zakat — karena aku hanya memiliki sepuluh unta yang merupakan kendaraan dan bekalku. Beliau menerima baiatku dengan pengecualian itu.'

309.iwh_en: It was narrated from Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya — the messenger of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ passed by a grave that was being filled and said: «Two rak'as that a person prays are more beloved to him than the world and everything in it.»'
309.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyah — utusan Rasulullah ﷺ — (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ melewati sebuah kuburan yang sedang ditimbun dan bersabda: «Dua rakaat yang seseorang shalat lebih dicintainya daripada dunia dan segala isinya.»'

310.iwh_en: It was narrated from Layla wife of Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that Bashir asked the Prophet ﷺ: 'Can I fast on Saturday?' He said: 'Observe the fast, but if you find nothing but a vine branch or a piece of twig, break your fast with it.'
310.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Layla istri Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Bashir bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ: 'Bolehkah aku berpuasa pada hari Sabtu?' Beliau bersabda: 'Berpuasalah, tetapi jika kamu tidak menemukan apapun kecuali ranting pohon anggur atau sepotong ranting, berbukalah dengannya.'

311.iwh_en: It was narrated from Layla wife of Bashir (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I wanted to fast two consecutive days, but Bashir prevented me and said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade continuous fasting.'
311.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Layla istri Bashir (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku ingin berpuasa dua hari berturut-turut, tetapi Bashir mencegahku dan berkata: Rasulullah ﷺ melarang puasa sambung-menyambung tanpa berbuka.'

312.iwh_en: It was narrated from Layla wife of Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya (may Allah be pleased with her) from Bashir that he came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, can I fast Saturday and Sunday?' He said: 'Yes, fast them as you wish.'
312.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Layla istri Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) dari Bashir bahwa ia datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, bolehkah aku berpuasa Sabtu dan Ahad?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ya, berpuasalah keduanya sesukamu.'

313.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Hanzala Ghusil al-Mala'ika (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A dirham of usury that a man knowingly consumes is worse with Allah than thirty-six acts of fornication.'
313.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Hanzalah Ghusil al-Mala'ikah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Satu dirham riba yang dimakan seorang laki-laki dengan sengaja lebih berat di sisi Allah daripada tiga puluh enam kali zina.'

314.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Hanzala ibn al-Rahib (may Allah be pleased with him) from Ka'b that he said: 'For me to commit thirty-three acts of fornication is more beloved to me than to consume a dirham of usury knowingly.'
314.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Hanzalah ibn al-Rahib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Ka'b bahwa ia berkata: 'Bagiku melakukan tiga puluh tiga kali zina lebih aku sukai daripada memakan satu dirham riba dengan sengaja.'

315.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Hanzala ibn al-Rahib (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man greeted the Prophet ﷺ after he had urinated but before he had performed ablution. The Prophet ﷺ did not return the greeting until he reached a wall, wiped his hands and face on it, and then returned the greeting.
315.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Hanzalah ibn al-Rahib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki mengucapkan salam kepada Nabi ﷺ setelah beliau kencing tetapi sebelum berwudhu. Nabi ﷺ tidak membalas salam itu hingga beliau mencapai sebuah dinding, mengusapkan tangan dan wajahnya di atasnya, kemudian membalas salam.'

316.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubaydullah ibn Abdullah ibn Umar that he asked a man about the ablution of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. The man said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution by washing his face once, his hands once, and wiping his head once, then washing his feet once — this was his ablution.'
316.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubaidullah ibn Abdullah ibn Umar bahwa ia bertanya kepada seorang laki-laki tentang wudhu Rasulullah ﷺ. Laki-laki itu berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ berwudhu dengan membasuh wajahnya sekali, tangannya sekali, dan mengusap kepalanya sekali, kemudian membasuh kakinya sekali — itulah wudhunya.'

317.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn Abdullah al-Khath'ami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever has his feet covered in dust in the way of Allah — Allah will forbid Hellfire from touching those feet.'
317.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn Abdullah al-Khath'ami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa kedua kakinya berdebu di jalan Allah — Allah akan mengharamkan api neraka menyentuh kedua kaki itu.'

318.iwh_en: It was narrated that a man was leading his horse in the ranks while calling to the commander Malik ibn Abdullah al-Khath'ami (may Allah be pleased with him). He said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: «The martyr in the way of Allah is forgiven at the first gush of blood, and he sees his place in Paradise.»'
318.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa seorang laki-laki menuntun kudanya di barisan sambil memanggil sang komandan Malik ibn Abdullah al-Khath'ami (radhiyallahu 'anhu). Ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: «Syahid di jalan Allah diampuni pada tetesan darah yang pertama, dan ia melihat tempatnya di surga.»'

319.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn Abdullah al-Khath'ami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever has his feet covered in dust in the way of Allah — Allah will forbid Hellfire from touching him.'
319.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn Abdullah al-Khath'ami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa kedua kakinya berdebu di jalan Allah — Allah akan mengharamkan api neraka menyentuhnya.'

320.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn Abdullah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I went on a military expedition with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and I heard him say: «Whoever has his feet covered in dust in the way of Allah, Allah will forbid Hellfire from touching him.»'
320.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn Abdullah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berperang bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dan aku mendengarnya bersabda: «Barangsiapa kedua kakinya berdebu di jalan Allah, Allah akan mengharamkan api neraka menyentuhnya.»'

321.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Prophet ﷺ say' — and a man asked him — 'Indeed there is food that I am reluctant to eat. Is there sin in it?' He said: 'Do not let any food cause you concern — eat or give it away.'
321.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda' — seorang laki-laki bertanya kepadanya — 'Sesungguhnya ada makanan yang aku segan untuk memakannya. Apakah ada dosa di dalamnya?' Beliau bersabda: 'Janganlah ada makanan yang membuatmu khawatir — makanlah atau berikan saja.'

322.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about the food of Christians. He said: «Let no food cause uncertainty in your chest that you are imitating Christians. As for whatever preoccupies you — eat it or leave it.»'
322.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ tentang makanan orang Nasrani. Beliau bersabda: «Janganlah ada makanan yang menimbulkan keraguan di dadamu bahwa kamu menyerupai orang Nasrani. Adapun apa yang membuatmu ragu — makanlah atau tinggalkan.»'

323.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Prophet ﷺ turn away from his right side and from his left side. And I saw him place this hand on his chest.' Yahya said: 'And I saw him place his right hand on his left hand in prayer.'
323.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Nabi ﷺ berpaling dari sisi kanannya dan dari sisi kirinya. Dan aku melihatnya meletakkan tangan ini di dadanya.' Yahya berkata: 'Dan aku melihatnya meletakkan tangan kanannya di atas tangan kirinya dalam shalat.'

324.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Prophet ﷺ placing his right hand over his left hand in prayer. And I saw him turn away from both his right and left sides.'
324.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Nabi ﷺ meletakkan tangan kanannya di atas tangan kirinya dalam shalat. Dan aku melihatnya berpaling dari kedua sisinya — kanan dan kiri.'

325.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: 'I asked him about the food of Christians. He said: «Let no food cause uncertainty in your chest — or unease — about something in which you resemble Christians. Give in charity from what is in your hand.»'
325.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepadanya tentang makanan orang Nasrani. Beliau bersabda: «Janganlah ada makanan yang menimbulkan keraguan — atau kegelisahan — di dadamu tentang sesuatu yang kamu menyerupai orang Nasrani di dalamnya. Bersedekahlah dari apa yang ada di tanganmu.»'

326.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hulb (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet ﷺ used to turn away from his left side and from his right side, and he would place one hand over the other in prayer.'
326.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hulb (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi ﷺ biasa berpaling dari sisi kirinya dan dari sisi kanannya, dan beliau meletakkan satu tangan di atas tangan lainnya dalam shalat.'

327.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Prophet ﷺ speak about charity and say: 'Let no one come on the Day of Resurrection carrying a camel on his neck bellowing, or a cow lowing, or a sheep bleating — saying: O Messenger of Allah, intercede for me. I will say: I conveyed the message to you.'
327.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Nabi ﷺ berbicara tentang sedekah dan bersabda: 'Janganlah seseorang datang pada hari Kiamat dengan membawa unta di lehernya yang bersuara, atau sapi yang melenguh, atau kambing yang mengembik — seraya berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, syafaatilah aku. Aku akan berkata: Aku telah menyampaikan pesan kepadamu.'

328.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I asked the Prophet ﷺ about the food of Christians. He said: «Let no food cause unease in your chest that you resemble Christians in. As for what concerns you of it — eat it or leave it.»'
328.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang makanan orang Nasrani. Beliau bersabda: «Janganlah ada makanan yang menimbulkan kegelisahan di dadamu bahwa kamu menyerupai orang Nasrani di dalamnya. Adapun apa yang membuatmu khawatir darinya — makanlah atau tinggalkan.»'

329.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hulb (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw him turn sometimes to his right and sometimes to his left.'
329.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hulb (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihatnya berpaling kadang ke kanannya dan kadang ke kirinya.'

330.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ about the food of Christians. He said: «Do not let any food cause unease in your heart — eat it or leave it.»'
330.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ tentang makanan orang Nasrani. Beliau bersabda: «Janganlah ada makanan yang menimbulkan kegelisahan di hatimu — makanlah atau tinggalkan.»'

331.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ turn away from both his sides — right and left.'
331.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ berpaling dari kedua sisinya — kanan dan kiri.'

332.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to lead us in prayer and he would take his left hand with his right hand.'
332.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mengimami kami dalam shalat dan beliau memegang tangan kirinya dengan tangan kanannya.'

333.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to lead us in prayer and he would hold his left hand with his right hand. And he would turn away from both sides — right and left.'
333.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mengimami kami dalam shalat dan beliau memegang tangan kirinya dengan tangan kanannya. Dan beliau berpaling dari kedua sisi — kanan dan kiri.'

334.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to lead us in prayer and he would hold his left hand with his right hand. And he would turn away on both his sides.'
334.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mengimami kami dalam shalat dan beliau memegang tangan kirinya dengan tangan kanannya. Dan beliau berpaling pada kedua sisinya.'

335.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) in a prophetic narration: 'Whatever resembles Christianity — let it not cause unease in your chest.'
335.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) secara marfu': 'Apapun yang menyerupai Kekristenan — janganlah ia menimbulkan kegelisahan di dadamu.'

336.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Prophet ﷺ — who mentioned charity — say: 'Let no one come on the Day of Resurrection with his property carried on his neck, then say: O Muhammad, intercede for me. I will say: I have no power for you — I already conveyed the message.'
336.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Nabi ﷺ — yang menyebutkan tentang sedekah — bersabda: 'Janganlah seseorang datang pada hari Kiamat dengan hartanya terbawa di lehernya, lalu berkata: Wahai Muhammad, syafaatilah aku. Aku akan berkata: Aku tidak memiliki kuasa untukmu — aku sudah menyampaikan pesannya.'

337.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I prayed with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he would turn away on both his sides.'
337.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku shalat bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dan beliau berpaling pada kedua sisinya.'

338.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he prayed with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ turn away to both sides from the prayer.
338.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia shalat bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dan melihat Rasulullah ﷺ berpaling ke kedua sisi saat selesai shalat.'

339.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ spoke about charity and said: 'Let no one come on the Day of Resurrection carrying a camel on his neck, calling out: O Messenger of Allah! I will say: I conveyed the message. I have no power over anything for you.'
339.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berbicara tentang sedekah dan bersabda: 'Janganlah seseorang datang pada hari Kiamat dengan membawa unta di lehernya, seraya berseru: Wahai Rasulullah! Aku akan berkata: Aku telah menyampaikan pesan. Aku tidak memiliki kuasa atas apapun untukmu.'

340.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb al-Ta'i from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ turn away sometimes to his right and sometimes to his left.'
340.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb al-Tha'i dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ berpaling kadang ke kanannya dan kadang ke kirinya.'

341.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qabisa ibn Hulb al-Ta'i from his father (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he finished the prayer, would turn away to his right and to his left.'
341.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qabishah ibn Hulb al-Tha'i dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ, ketika selesai shalat, berpaling ke kanannya dan ke kirinya.'

342.iwh_en: It was narrated from Matar ibn 'Ukamis (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When Allah decrees a person's death in a particular land, He creates for him a need in that land.'
342.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mathar ibn 'Ukamis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila Allah menetapkan kematian seseorang di suatu tempat, Dia menciptakan baginya suatu kebutuhan di tempat itu.'

343.iwh_en: It was narrated from Matar ibn 'Ukamis (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whenever it is decreed that someone shall die in a certain land, Allah creates for him a cause to go there.'
343.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mathar ibn 'Ukamis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Setiap kali ditetapkan bahwa seseorang akan meninggal di suatu tempat, Allah menciptakan baginya sebab untuk pergi ke sana.'

344.iwh_en: It was narrated from Maymun ibn Sunbad — a Companion of the Prophet ﷺ (may Allah be pleased with him) — that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: «The best of what a man leaves behind are three: a righteous child who supplicates for him, an ongoing charity that continues to be rewarded after him, and knowledge that benefits those after him.»'
344.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Maimun ibn Sunbad — seorang sahabat Nabi ﷺ (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: «Sebaik-baik yang ditinggalkan seorang laki-laki ada tiga: anak yang shalih yang mendoakannya, sedekah yang mengalir yang terus diberi pahala setelahnya, dan ilmu yang bermanfaat bagi orang-orang setelahnya.»'

345.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that when he returned from Yemen he said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I saw men in Yemen prostrating to each other — should we not prostrate to you?' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'No. If I were to command anyone to prostrate to another human being, I would have commanded women to prostrate to their husbands, due to the greatness of the right that husbands have over them.'
345.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika ia kembali dari Yaman ia berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, aku melihat laki-laki di Yaman bersujud satu sama lain — tidakkah kita bersujud kepadamu?' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak. Seandainya aku memerintahkan seseorang bersujud kepada manusia lain, niscaya aku perintahkan perempuan bersujud kepada suami mereka, karena besarnya hak suami atas mereka.'

346.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'O Mu'adh, follow a bad deed with a good deed — it will erase it. And treat people with good character.'
346.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepadanya: 'Wahai Mu'adz, ikutilah keburukan dengan kebaikan — ia akan menghapusnya. Dan pergauilah manusia dengan akhlak yang baik.'

347.iwh_en: It was narrated from Musa ibn Talha that he said: 'We have the book of Mu'adh from the Prophet ﷺ stating that he only took zakat from wheat, barley, dates, and raisins.'
347.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Musa ibn Thalhah bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami memiliki kitab Mu'adz dari Nabi ﷺ yang menyatakan bahwa beliau hanya mengambil zakat dari gandum, barley, kurma, dan kismis.'

348.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent me to Arab villages and commanded me to take the share of the land — meaning a tithe from rain-watered crops and half a tithe from irrigated crops.'
348.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mengirimku ke desa-desa Arab dan memerintahkanku untuk mengambil bagian dari tanah — yaitu sepersepuluh dari tanaman yang diairi hujan dan seperduapuluh dari tanaman yang diairi dengan irigasi.'

349.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was riding behind the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and he said: «O Mu'adh, do you know what is Allah's right over His servants?» I said: Allah and His Messenger know best. He said: «Allah's right over His servants is that they worship Him and associate nothing with Him. And do you know what the servants' right over Allah is?» I said: Allah and His Messenger know best. He said: «The servants' right over Allah is that He does not punish the one who associates nothing with Him.»'
349.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku sedang membonceng Rasulullah ﷺ dan beliau bersabda: «Wahai Mu'adz, tahukah kamu apa hak Allah atas hamba-hamba-Nya?» Aku berkata: Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih tahu. Beliau bersabda: «Hak Allah atas hamba-hamba-Nya adalah bahwa mereka menyembah-Nya dan tidak menyekutukan-Nya dengan apapun. Dan tahukah kamu apa hak hamba-hamba atas Allah?» Aku berkata: Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih tahu. Beliau bersabda: «Hak hamba-hamba atas Allah adalah bahwa Dia tidak mengazab orang yang tidak menyekutukan-Nya.»'

350.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Six signs of the Hour: my death, then the conquest of Bayt al-Maqdis, then a death that will seize the people like the pest among sheep, then wealth will increase until a man is given a hundred dinars and he is still angry, then a tribulation that does not leave any Arab household without entering it, then a truce between you and Banu Asfar — and they will betray you.'
350.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Enam tanda hari Kiamat: kematianku, kemudian penaklukan Bayt al-Maqdis, kemudian kematian yang akan menyerang manusia seperti wabah pada domba, kemudian harta akan berlimpah hingga seorang laki-laki diberi seratus dinar dan ia masih marah, kemudian fitnah yang tidak meninggalkan rumah tangga Arab manapun kecuali memasukinya, kemudian gencatan senjata antara kalian dan Banu Ashfar — dan mereka akan mengkhianati kalian.'
